name: sh

globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Display version information and exit

globalOptions.name: --help
globalOptions.description: Display usage information and exit

globalOptions.name: -c
globalOptions.description: Execute commands from the following string argument

globalOptions.name: -i
globalOptions.description: Start an interactive shell

globalOptions.name: -l
globalOptions.description: Act as if invoked as a login shell (same as --login)

globalOptions.name: --login
globalOptions.description: Act as if invoked as a login shell

globalOptions.name: -r
globalOptions.description: Start a restricted shell

globalOptions.name: --restricted
globalOptions.description: Start a restricted shell

globalOptions.name: -s
globalOptions.description: Read commands from standard input

globalOptions.name: -D
globalOptions.description: Print double-quoted strings preceded by $ (for localization)

globalOptions.name: --dump-strings
globalOptions.description: Same as -D

globalOptions.name: -e
globalOptions.description: Exit immediately if a command exits with non-zero status (set -e)

globalOptions.name: -u
globalOptions.description: Treat unset variables as an error (set -u)

globalOptions.name: -x
globalOptions.description: Print each command before executing it (set -x)

globalOptions.name: -v
globalOptions.description: Print shell input lines as they are read (set -v)

globalOptions.name: -n
globalOptions.description: Read commands but do not execute them (syntax check)

globalOptions.name: -p
globalOptions.description: Start in privileged mode

globalOptions.name: --posix
globalOptions.description: Change behavior to match the POSIX standard

globalOptions.name: --norc
globalOptions.description: Do not read the ~/.bashrc initialization file

globalOptions.name: --noprofile
globalOptions.description: Do not read /etc/profile or any personal profile files

globalOptions.name: --rcfile
globalOptions.description: Execute commands from specified file instead of ~/.bashrc

globalOptions.name: --init-file
globalOptions.description: Same as --rcfile

globalOptions.name: -O
globalOptions.description: Enable a shell option (shopt)

globalOptions.name: +O
globalOptions.description: Disable a shell option (shopt)

globalOptions.name: --debugger
globalOptions.description: Run the debugger profile before the shell starts

globalOptions.name: --dump-po-strings
globalOptions.description: Same as -D but output in GNU gettext PO format

globalOptions.name: --verbose
globalOptions.description: Same as -v

globalOptions.name: --noediting
globalOptions.description: Do not use the GNU Readline library for command-line editing

builtinCommands.name: alias
builtinCommands.description: Define or display aliases

builtinCommands.name: bg
builtinCommands.description: Resume a suspended job in the background

builtinCommands.name: cd
builtinCommands.description: Change the current directory

builtinCommands.name: declare
builtinCommands.description: Declare variables and give them attributes

builtinCommands.name: echo
builtinCommands.description: Display a line of text

builtinCommands.name: eval
builtinCommands.description: Concatenate arguments and execute as a shell command

builtinCommands.name: exec
builtinCommands.description: Replace the shell with the given command

builtinCommands.name: exit
builtinCommands.description: Exit the shell with a status

builtinCommands.name: export
builtinCommands.description: Set environment variables for child processes

builtinCommands.name: fg
builtinCommands.description: Resume a suspended job in the foreground

builtinCommands.name: getopts
builtinCommands.description: Parse positional parameters as options

builtinCommands.name: hash
builtinCommands.description: Remember the full pathname of commands

builtinCommands.name: history
builtinCommands.description: Display or manipulate the history list

builtinCommands.name: jobs
builtinCommands.description: Display status of jobs

builtinCommands.name: kill
builtinCommands.description: Send a signal to a process

builtinCommands.name: let
builtinCommands.description: Evaluate arithmetic expressions

builtinCommands.name: local
builtinCommands.description: Define local variables in a function

builtinCommands.name: printf
builtinCommands.description: Format and print data

builtinCommands.name: pushd
builtinCommands.description: Save current directory and change to another

builtinCommands.name: popd
builtinCommands.description: Restore previously saved directory

builtinCommands.name: read
builtinCommands.description: Read a line from standard input

builtinCommands.name: readonly
builtinCommands.description: Mark variables as read-only

builtinCommands.name: return
builtinCommands.description: Return from a shell function

builtinCommands.name: set
builtinCommands.description: Set or unset shell options and positional parameters

builtinCommands.name: shift
builtinCommands.description: Shift positional parameters to the left

builtinCommands.name: shopt
builtinCommands.description: Toggle shell optional behavior

builtinCommands.name: source
builtinCommands.description: Execute commands from a file in the current shell

builtinCommands.name: test
builtinCommands.description: Evaluate conditional expression

builtinCommands.name: trap
builtinCommands.description: Set actions to execute when signals are received

builtinCommands.name: type
builtinCommands.description: Display information about command type

builtinCommands.name: typeset
builtinCommands.description: Declare variables and give them attributes (synonym for declare)

builtinCommands.name: ulimit
builtinCommands.description: Set or display user resource limits

builtinCommands.name: umask
builtinCommands.description: Display or set the file mode creation mask

builtinCommands.name: unalias
builtinCommands.description: Remove alias definitions

builtinCommands.name: unset
builtinCommands.description: Remove variable or function names

builtinCommands.name: wait
builtinCommands.description: Wait for a process to complete

examples: bash -c 'for i in 1 2 3; do echo $i; done'

detectors.name: oh_my_zsh
detectors.description: Check if Oh My Zsh is installed
